name: Math Competition — Boxed Answers
description: Original competition-style math problems (algebra, combinatorics, number theory) each with a single closed-form final answer to be placed in \boxed{}. Inspired by MATH-500.

items.prompt: Find the last two digits of $7^{2025}$. Put your final answer in \boxed{}.
How many ordered triples of positive integers $(x, y, z)$ with $x < y < z$ satisfy $x + y + z = 20$? Put your final answer in \boxed{}.
Two fair six-sided dice are rolled. What is the probability that the sum of the two numbers shown is a prime number? Express your answer as a common fraction and put it in \boxed{}.
Evaluate the sum $\displaystyle\sum_{k=1}^{99} \frac{1}{k(k+1)}$. Express your answer as a common fraction and put it in \boxed{}.
What is the largest integer $k$ such that $2^k$ divides $50!$? Put your final answer in \boxed{}.
How many distinct arrangements are there of the letters in the word MISSISSIPPI? Put your final answer in \boxed{}.
Let $r$ and $s$ be the roots of $x^2 - 5x + 6 = 0$. Compute $r^3 + s^3$. Put your final answer in \boxed{}.
Find the last two digits of $1! + 2! + 3! + \cdots + 100!$. Put your final answer in \boxed{}.
Evaluate $\displaystyle\sum_{k=1}^{100} \lfloor \sqrt{k} \rfloor$, where $\lfloor x \rfloor$ denotes the greatest integer not exceeding $x$. Put your final answer in \boxed{}.
What is the sum of all two-digit positive integers that are divisible by 7? Put your final answer in \boxed{}.
When $x^{100}$ is divided by $x^2 - 1$, the remainder can be written as $ax + b$. Find $a + b$. Put your final answer in \boxed{}.
